rationale: The Iran-US peace deal signal and Hormuz reopening prospect could ease energy prices for Poland; EU accession launch for Ukraine/Moldova continues as background positive — no major new escalation on the eastern front this cycle.

top_events.title: Iran signals Hormuz reopening if deal reached
top_events.summary: A US-Iran peace deal could reopen the Strait of Hormuz, easing global energy prices and reducing Poland's energy costs indirectly. Situation not yet signed — still developing.

top_events.title: EU formally launches accession process for Ukraine and Moldova
top_events.summary: EU accession talks officially launched for Ukraine and Moldova — a geopolitically significant step for Poland's eastern neighbors, reinforcing regional stability long-term.

top_events.title: UAE to unlock frozen Iranian funds amid US ceasefire push
top_events.summary: Unfreezing Iranian assets signals momentum toward a US-Iran deal, which could further ease oil market pressure and energy costs relevant to Poland.
